section_title: Alternative height and setback - qualifying residential sites (R1 through R5)

source.document_currency_banner_note: City of Yes for Housing Opportunity moved the R1-R5 residential bulk (height and setback) regulations into the ZR 23-42 series with a Last Amended date of 12/5/2024. The site-wide currency banner was not captured for this retrieval and is a needs_review item.
source.verification_required: Draft capture of ZR 23-424 supplied by official-source-researcher (access 2026-07-22, zr.planning.nyc.gov). It has NOT been re-verified byte-for-byte against raw HTML by the rules-engineer, so raw_html_verified is false and extraction_status is extracted_draft. The governing section, the exact heights/depths, the district-to-statement association, and every applicability condition (building type, street width, qualifying-residential-site geography, special-district/overlay/historic-district context) MUST be confirmed by a qualified zoning professional (G6) before any rule citing this snapshot is published or Verified.

verbatim_excerpt: R5 R5A R5B R5D | Maximum Base Height 45 | Maximum Height of Buildings or other Structures 55

columns: Maximum Height of Buildings or other Structures
table.table_note: The §23-424 alternative (taller) envelope - base height 45 ft, building height 55 ft, with a setback per §23-423 - applies ONLY on a 'qualifying residential site' (§12-10): broadly a lot of 5,000 sf or more within the Greater Transit Zone with wide-street/short-block frontage and no excluding district. That geography cannot be evaluated from the canonical property_profile, so the alternative fails closed to professional_review_required.

notes: Alternative envelope for all four R5 variants on a qualifying residential site. Whether a lot qualifies is a separate legal determination (§12-10) NOT decided by this snapshot or any agent.
Captured for M4-T006 as a governing-source candidate for the R5-series height & setback DRAFT rule family (needs_review).
Nothing in this record is a Verified zoning determination. Verified requires an M4 published rule plus G6 qualified-human approval (owner directive item 5).
